_comment: Note: userPromptSubmitted is intentionally omitted. Per docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/hooks-configuration, Copilot does not consume hook stdout for that event ('Output processed: No'), so route-prompt's natural-language routing would be a silent no-op. Claude Code, which DOES honor UserPromptSubmit stdout, still runs route-prompt via .claude/settings.json. Copilot also discards sessionStart stdout, so NO hook can inject routing context on Copilot -- routing there rests entirely on AGENTS.md > Agentic Workflow (section 1), the always-on instruction surface. preToolUse IS consumed (JSON permissionDecision deny), which is how guard.* still enforces hard-blocks on Copilot. See docs/enforcement-surfaces.md for the guaranteed-vs-instructed matrix. The per-tool 'matcher' field is intentionally omitted (Copilot supports it, but): VS Code agent mode emits write tool names that cannot be fully enumerated (camelCase str_replace/insert/etc.), so guard.* must see every tool call and self-filter on file-path+content -- a 'matcher' regex like 'edit|create' would blind it to those VS Code writes and open a security gap. The fast self-exit on non-write calls is the deliberate cost of that coverage; do not add a matcher here.
